commands.review_summary:
Adds a static moderation reason for extreme whitespace/context-padding evasion, scans text artifacts for padding before later content, adds regression tests, and records the ClawScan intent in the moderation spec.

Reproducibility: yes. the source-level reproduction path is high-confidence: scan a text artifact with visible content, thousands of blank lines or a 40,000-character whitespace run, and later content. I did not execute tests in this read-only review, but the branch adds focused unit cases for that shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep this PR in human review: there is no narrow code defect for automation to repair, but the external contribution needs real behavior proof and maintainer threshold review before merge.

target.title: Flag context-padding scan evasion
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a deterministic static scan signal for context-padding evasion in skill artifacts.

The reported bypass shape is simple: put normal-looking `SKILL.md` content near the top, add a very large run of blank lines or whitespace, and place the real payload after that padding. ClawHub's publish static scan already reads the full text file, but it did not treat the padding itself as suspicious. That meant an artifact could hide important content from context-limited previews or model review without creating a deterministic static finding.

This PR makes that evasive layout visible to ClawScan.

## What changed

- Adds `suspicious.context_padding_truncation` as a static scan reason code.
- Flags extreme blank-line runs before later artifact content.
- Flags extreme same-line whitespace runs before later artifact content.
- Keeps the signal as static evidence for ClawScan, not a standalone hard block or uploader moderation action.
- Adds focused regression tests for blank-line padding, long whitespace padding, and ordinary sparse markdown that should remain clean.
- Updates the moderation spec to record the intent: this is context-evasion evidence because it can hide payloads from context-limited review.

## What this prevents

This prevents a padded skill from looking clean to the deterministic static layer just because the suspicious content is pushed far below a large whitespace run. A 100,000-newline artifact now produces a clear `suspicious.context_padding_truncation` finding that points reviewers and ClawScan at the evasion attempt.

## Validation

- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/moderationEngine.test.ts`
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run ci:unit`

Manual repro: the original 100,000-newline shape now returns `suspicious` with `suspicious.context_padding_truncation`.

Codex `/review` was not run, by request.
